title: Syngramma hosei
intro: Syngramma hosei, commonly known as Indian pipeplant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Syngramma hosei include glycosides, flavonoids, and other phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat conditions such as skin infections, fever, and respiratory issues.
